description: Athom Smart Plug v3 (PG02-V3) with an ESP32-C3 module, 16 A onboard relay, HLW8012 energy metering, push button, and status LED. Sold pre-flashed with ESPHome firmware. Popular drop-in replacement for Sonoff/TP-Link plugs in Home Assistant deployments.

featured_bundles.description:
Adds the onboard LED's GPIO output plus a binary light entity so it
surfaces as a light in Home Assistant.
featured_bundles.name: Status LED (full setup)

featured_components.description:
16 A relay hard-wired to GPIO6. The pin is fixed; the rest of the
fields are yours to set.
featured_components.name: Onboard Relay

featured_components.description:
Front-panel push button on GPIO9 (active low — uses internal pull-up
via the rich pin form).
featured_components.name: Front-Panel Button

featured_components.description:
Low-level GPIO output for the onboard blue LED on GPIO7. Pair with
a ``Status LED Light`` so Home Assistant can toggle it as a light.
featured_components.name: Status LED Output

featured_components.description:
Binary light entity for the onboard status LED. Wire its ``output``
to the ``status_led_output`` you added.
featured_components.name: Status LED Light

featured_components.description:
Energy-metering chip wired to GPIO3 (CF), GPIO4 (CF1), and GPIO5
(SEL). The pins are physically fixed on this board.
featured_components.name: HLW8012 Power Monitor

name: Athom Smart Plug v3

pins.label: HLW_CF
pins.notes: Pulse output proportional to active power

pins.label: HLW_SEL
pins.notes: Selects whether CF1 reports current or voltage

pins.label: RELAY
pins.notes: Active high — drives the front-panel relay

pins.label: STATUS_LED
pins.notes: Active low — must be inverted for "on" = LED on

pins.label: BUTTON
pins.notes: Boot strapping pin — wired to the front-panel button (active low)
